entry.title: Re: Implementation Process Insights,Discovery Edition
entry.content:
Hi

To request the Process Insights, Discovery Edition service for a prospective client as a partner, you typically need to follow a structured process within SAP's partner ecosystem. Here are the general steps to initiate the request and obtain the necessary SAP report:

Steps to Request Process Insights, Discovery Edition as a Partner:

1. Partner Enablement and Access
- Ensure that your organization is enrolled as an SAP partner and has access to the SAP PartnerEdge portal. This portal provides resources and tools for partners to manage engagements and request SAP products.

2. Engage with SAP Account Executive
- Contact the SAP Account Executive responsible for your region or the specific SAP product line (Process Insights, in this case).
- Discuss the client's interest in Process Insights, Discovery Edition and gather initial details about the client’s requirements.

3. Requesting the Product
- Submit a request through the SAP PartnerEdge portal or directly to your SAP contact. The request should include details about the client, their industry, specific use cases for Process Insights, and any other relevant information.
- Clearly outline why the client would benefit from using Process Insights and how it aligns with their business objectives.

4. Product Demonstration and Evaluation
- Arrange a product demonstration for the client with SAP or your SAP Account Executive.
- Ensure the client understands the capabilities of Process Insights, Discovery Edition and how it can add value to their business processes.

5. Obtaining the SAP Report
- Once the client agrees to proceed with Process Insights, Discovery Edition, SAP will provide access to the necessary tools and resources.
- SAP will generate the required reports based on the analysis of the client's business processes using Process Insights.

6. Implementation and Support
- Assist the client in implementing Process Insights, if necessary, or provide support during the evaluation phase.
- Address any client questions or concerns regarding the product or the generated SAP report.

Additional Considerations:

- Documentation and Training Ensure your team is familiar with the features and benefits of Process Insights, Discovery Edition to effectively communicate its value to clients.
- Follow-Up and Feedback Maintain regular communication with SAP and the client to address any issues or feedback during the evaluation or implementation phase.
- Sales and Licensing Understand the licensing model for Process Insights and ensure proper agreements are in place with the client.

By following these steps and leveraging your partnership with SAP, you can effectively request Process Insights, Discovery Edition for your prospective clients and facilitate the evaluation process to demonstrate its value in optimizing their business processes. If you encounter specific challenges or need further guidance, consulting with your SAP Partner Executive or SAP Partner Support can provide additional assistance tailored to your partnership and client engagement needs.

entry.title: Re: Implementation Process Insights,Discovery Edition
entry.content:
Hi 

Few more solution:

Certainly! Here’s a more structured example of the steps and format you might follow as a partner to request Process Insights, Discovery Edition for a prospective client:

Example Steps to Request Process Insights, Discovery Edition

1. Partner Contact Information:
- Partner Name: [Your Company Name]
- Partner ID: [Your Partner ID]
- Partner Contact: [Your Contact Person]
- Contact Email: [Your Email]
- Contact Phone: [Your Phone Number]

2. Client Information:
- Client Name: [Client Company Name]
- Industry: [Client Industry]
- Contact Person at Client: [Client Contact Person]
- Client Email: [Client Email]
- Client Phone: [Client Phone Number]

3. Engagement Details:
- Purpose of Engagement: Request for Process Insights, Discovery Edition evaluation.
- Description of Client’s Needs/Challenges: [Brief description of why the client is interested in Process Insights, e.g., process optimization, efficiency improvement, compliance validation, etc.]

4. Use Case and Business Objectives:
- Use Case: Describe how Process Insights, Discovery Edition aligns with the client’s business objectives.
- Expected Benefits: Outline the expected benefits for the client, such as cost savings, time efficiency, or compliance improvements.

5. Requested Actions:
- Product Request: Request access to Process Insights, Discovery Edition for client evaluation.
- Demonstration: Request a product demonstration for the client to showcase capabilities and benefits.
- SAP Report: Request the SAP report based on the analysis of the client’s business processes using Process Insights.

6. Next Steps:
- Coordinate with SAP Account Executive to arrange a demonstration.
- Follow up on the request status and timeline for accessing Process Insights.
- Prepare to assist the client during the evaluation phase and address any questions or concerns.

7. Additional Information:
- Include any additional relevant information or specific requirements for the client engagement.

---

Example Request Email Format:

```
Subject: Request for Process Insights, Discovery Edition Evaluation

Dear [SAP Account Executive Name],

I hope this message finds you well. We are reaching out on behalf of [Your Company Name], an SAP partner, with a request to evaluate Process Insights, Discovery Edition for one of our prospective clients, [Client Company Name].

Partner Contact Information:
- Partner Name: [Your Company Name]
- Partner ID: [Your Partner ID]
- Partner Contact: [Your Contact Person]
- Contact Email: [Your Email]
- Contact Phone: [Your Phone Number]

Client Information:
- Client Name: [Client Company Name]
- Industry: [Client Industry]
- Contact Person at Client: [Client Contact Person]
- Client Email: [Client Email]
- Client Phone: [Client Phone Number]

Engagement Details:
- Purpose of Engagement: Request for Process Insights, Discovery Edition evaluation.
- Description of Client’s Needs/Challenges: [Brief description of why the client is interested in Process Insights]

Use Case and Business Objectives:
- Use Case: Describe how Process Insights, Discovery Edition aligns with the client’s business objectives.
- Expected Benefits: Outline the expected benefits for the client, such as cost savings, time efficiency, or compliance improvements.

Requested Actions:
- Product Request: Request access to Process Insights, Discovery Edition for client evaluation.
- Demonstration: Request a product demonstration for the client.
- SAP Report: Request the SAP report based on the analysis of client’s business processes using Process Insights.

Next Steps:
- Coordinate with you to arrange a demonstration for the client.
- Follow up on the request status and timeline for accessing Process Insights.
- Prepare to assist the client during the evaluation phase and address any questions or concerns.

Additional Information:
- [Any additional information or specific requirements]

We look forward to your guidance and support in facilitating this evaluation. Please let us know if you require any further information or if there are specific forms or processes we should follow.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Position]
[Your Company Name]
[Your Contact Information]
```

---

This format provides a structured approach to requesting Process Insights, Discovery Edition for a client, ensuring clarity in communication and alignment with SAP’s processes. Adjust the details as per your specific partnership and client engagement context.

entry.title: Dividing different units in advanced formula
entry.content:
Hallo,

i try to divide two measures. One is currency EUR and the other one Unit FTE.

This is just a stripped down version of my script to show the problem.

I have implemented to version to calculate the @average - line 9 and line 17

when i uncomment line 17 i get the correct result of 30(see screenshot)

in the commented version(this is my prefered one) i get 0.

It seems to have something to do with units because when i store the results in separate variables i get the expected result.

The FLOAT()s i added to the calculation in line 9 but with no effect.

Thanks for any ideas

MEMBERSET [d/Account] = ("Opex - Salaries & Wages", "FHeads_FJEmps")
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "202411" TO "202412"
MEMBERSET [d/PD_Business_Area] = ("CSO")
MEMBERSET [d/PD_Cost_Center] = ("1000/#")
FLOAT @averagefte
FLOAT @averagesal
FLOAT @average

@average =
FLOAT(
	   	FLOAT(RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages", [d/Date] = "202411")) /
		FLOAT(RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "FHeads_FJEmps", [d/Date] = "202411"))
	  )

@averagesal = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages", [d/Date] = "202411")
@averagefte = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "FHeads_FJEmps", [d/Date] = "202411")
//@average = @averagesal / @averagefte

DATA([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages", [d/Date] = "202412", [d/PD_Business_Area] = "CSO") = ((RESULTLOOKUP([d/Date] = "202411", [d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages", [d/PD_Business_Area] = "CSO")) * 0) + @average

entry.title: Re: I am looking for Get & Post methods for custom Api fo...
entry.content:
Hi 

To maintain custom validations for GET and POST methods in a custom API on a public cloud tenant, such as SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), you typically need to develop and deploy your API using the appropriate tools and services provided by the platform. Here’s a guide to help you navigate where and how to maintain your code for custom validations:

Steps to Maintain Custom Validations in a Custom API

#1. Choose Development Environment

- SAP Cloud Platform: Determine whether you are developing your API within the Cloud Foundry environment using Node.js, Java, or another supported language.
- SAP Business Application Studio (formerly SAP Web IDE): This integrated development environment (IDE) can be used for developing applications and services that interact with SAP BTP APIs.

#2. Create API Project

- Cloud Foundry: Create a new project or application where you will define your API endpoints.
- SAP API Management: If you are using API Management for API governance, you will define policies and logic for validation and security here.

#3. Define API Endpoints

- GET Method: Implement logic to fetch data from backend systems, databases, or other APIs. Validate parameters and handle edge cases.

Example in Node.js using Express:
```javascript
app.get('/api/resource', (req, res) => {
// Validate parameters
const { id } = req.query;
if (!id) {
return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Parameter id is required.' });
}
// Fetch data
// Perform custom validations or business logic
// Return response
res.json({ id, message: 'Data fetched successfully.' });
});
```

- POST Method: Implement logic to handle incoming data, validate inputs, and process business logic accordingly.

Example in Node.js using Express:
```javascript
app.post('/api/resource', (req, res) => {
// Validate incoming data
const { name, email } = req.body;
if (!name || !email) {
return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Name and email are required.' });
}
// Process data, e.g., save to database
// Perform additional custom validations
// Return response
res.json({ message: 'Data saved successfully.' });
});
```

#4. Implement Custom Validations

- Input Validation: Ensure that incoming parameters (query parameters for GET requests, request body for POST requests) are validated to prevent malicious input or errors.
- Business Logic: Apply business rules specific to your application or API operations.
- Error Handling: Implement error handling to gracefully manage exceptions and provide meaningful responses to clients.

#5. Deploy and Test

- Deployment: Deploy your API application to the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry environment or relevant service.
- Testing: Use tools like Postman or the SAP API Business Hub to test your API endpoints thoroughly, including testing for validations and error scenarios.

#6. Monitor and Maintain

- Monitoring: Monitor your API performance and usage to identify any issues related to validations or error handling.
- Maintenance: Regularly update and maintain your API codebase to ensure it remains secure and compliant with changing business requirements.

Example Tools and Resources

- SAP Business Application Studio: Integrated development environment for developing and deploying applications on SAP BTP.
- SAP API Management: For governance, security policies, and managing API lifecycle.
- Postman: API testing tool for testing API endpoints and validating responses.

Conclusion

By following these steps and leveraging the development tools and services provided by SAP BTP, you can effectively maintain custom validations in your GET and POST methods for custom APIs on a public cloud tenant. Adapt these steps based on your specific requirements, development environment, and the capabilities of SAP BTP services you are using. If you need further assistance, consulting SAP documentation or reaching out to SAP support can provide additional guidance tailored to your needs.

entry.title: Re: Custom value help for Custom Business Object in SAP S...
entry.content:
Hi 

Adding custom value helps for custom business objects in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud involves several steps and considerations, especially when integrating with standard SAP objects like Materials. Here’s a structured approach and considerations based on your scenario:

Steps to Implement Custom Value Help for a Custom Business Object

#1. Define Custom Business Object

- Ensure your custom business object is properly defined in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using SAP Fiori Elements or SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP).

#2. Integration with Standard Value Help (e.g., Material)

- Purpose: You want to utilize the standard value help for Material in your custom business object.
- Configuration: Follow the blog post you referenced for setting up the value help. Here’s a summary of steps to ensure everything is correctly configured:

#3. Configuration Steps

- Define Value Help Annotation:
- In your custom business object definition, annotate the attribute or field where Material information is required with the appropriate annotations (`@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition`, `@Common.ValueList`).

- Service Implementation:
- Ensure your OData service or API implementation exposes the necessary data for the value help. This typically involves:
- Defining or extending an existing OData service to include the required entity sets and navigation properties.
- Implementing logic to fetch and provide data for the Material value help.

- Annotation Examples:
- Annotate your custom business object’s CDS view or OData service metadata with annotations like `@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition` and `@Common.ValueList`.

#4. Testing and Validation

- Test in Development Environment: Ensure that in your development or sandbox environment, the value help for Material is correctly fetching data.
- Verify Integration: Test the integration of the Material value help within your custom business object’s UI or application.

#5. Considerations

- Authorization and Data Visibility: Ensure that users have appropriate authorizations to view Materials and that data visibility aligns with organizational policies.
- Custom Logic: If additional logic or filters are required for the Material value help (e.g., restricting by plant or storage location), implement these in your service logic.

#6. Documentation and Support

- SAP Community: Engage with the SAP Community forums or blogs for specific questions or issues related to integrating value helps in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
- SAP Help Portal: Refer to SAP’s official documentation and guides for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, particularly around custom business object configuration and value helps.

Example Scenario

Assume you have a custom business object for "Sales Orders" (`ZSalesOrder`) and you want to provide a value help for the Material field:

- Annotation Example:
```abap
@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [
{
@Common.ValueList: {
label: 'Material',
collectionPath: '/Materials',
keyProperty: 'MaterialID',
descriptionProperty: 'MaterialDescription'
}
}
]
MaterialID: String(10);
```

- Ensure that `/Materials` is exposed correctly in your OData service and that it retrieves Material data from the SAP S/4HANA backend.

Conclusion:Implementing custom value helps for custom business objects in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud requires careful configuration and integration with existing SAP standard objects like Materials. Follow the steps outlined above, and refer to SAP’s documentation and community resources for detailed guidance specific to your SAP S/4HANA version and deployment. If issues persist, leverage SAP support or consult with SAP partners who specialize in SAP S/4HANA customization and integration.

entry.title: SAP SLT Create time stamp wiped out with record update.
entry.content:
Dear Team,

i need a small help in modifing the code.

i have created a following incude program to fetch 'Create time stamp' ( zcolumn CRTTTM) in the Target HANDB when a record is replicated or inserted (IUUC_OPERAT_FLAG =I) . This is working fine.

However when the same record is updated (IUUC_OPERAT_FLAG  = 'U) the zcolumn (CRTTTM) with create timestamp is wiped out.

we want to hold this value

CODE/include: 

DATA: lv_name_create(30) TYPE c.
STATICS lv_Date TYPE sy-datum.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <ls_record> TYPE any,
<ls_crt_record> TYPE any,
<lv_crttm> TYPE any,
<lv_operation> TYPE any.

CONCATENATE '<wa_r_' i_p1 '>' INTO lv_name_create.
ASSIGN (lv_name_create) TO <ls_record>.

ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IUUC_OPERAT_FLAG' OF STRUCTURE <ls_record> TO <lv_operation>.

CASE <lv_operation>.
WHEN 'I'.
*** set additional target fields
* set insert timestamp
ASSIGN COMPONENT 'CRTTTM' OF STRUCTURE <ls_record> TO <lv_crttm>.
IF sy-subrc = 0.
CONCATENATE sy-datum sy-uzeit INTO <lv_crttm>.
lv_date = <lv_crttm>.
ENDIF.
WHEN OTHERS.
ASSIGN COMPONENT 'CRTTTM' OF STRUCTURE <ls_record> TO <lv_crttm>.
IF sy-subrc = 0.
<lv_crttm> = lv_date.
ENDIF.
ENDCASE.

entry.title: Re: Dividing different units in advanced formula
entry.content:
Hi

It seems like you're working with SAP Business Planning and Consolidation (BPC) script logic to calculate averages for two measures: currency (EUR) and Unit (FTE). Based on your description, the issue arises when you try to directly divide the results within the `@average` calculation, but not when you store them in separate variables (`@averagesal` and `@averagefte`). This could indeed be related to the data types and how BPC handles unit conversions or operations involving different units.

Here’s a structured approach to address and potentially resolve the issue:

Understanding the Issue

The issue seems to be related to how BPC handles operations involving measures with different units (currency vs. unit). BPC is stringent about unit consistency in calculations, especially when performing direct arithmetic operations.

Steps to Resolve the Issue

1. Separate Calculation Approach:
- Since storing results in separate variables (`@averagesal` and `@averagefte`) gives you the expected result, consider using this approach instead of trying to directly calculate `@average` using division.

```plaintext
@averagesal = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages", [d/Date] = "202411")
@averagefte = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "FHeads_FJEmps", [d/Date] = "202411")
@average = @averagesal / @averagefte
```

2. Unit Conversion and Data Types:
- Ensure that the data types (`EUR` for currency and `FTE` for units) are correctly handled in your BPC environment.
- If needed, use BPC functions or logic to convert units before performing calculations, although this may not be directly supported depending on your setup.

3. Check for Unit Mismatches:
- Double-check if there are any mismatches or inconsistencies in how units (`EUR` and `FTE`) are defined or handled in your BPC model or script logic.

4. Debugging and Testing:
- Test your script logic with different scenarios and sample data to understand how BPC processes the calculations.
- Use debug modes or trace functionality in BPC to identify any specific errors or warnings related to unit conversions or arithmetic operations.

Example Adjusted Script

Here’s how you might adjust your script based on the separate calculation approach:

```plaintext
MEMBERSET [d/Account] = ("Opex - Salaries & Wages", "FHeads_FJEmps")
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "202411"
FLOAT @averagesal
FLOAT @averagefte
FLOAT @average

@averagesal = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages", [d/Date] = "202411")
@averagefte = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "FHeads_FJEmps", [d/Date] = "202411")
@average = @averagesal / @averagefte
```

Conclusion:By following these steps and considerations, you can address the issue of calculating averages for measures with different units in SAP BPC script logic. Ensure that you adhere to BPC’s handling of units and data types, and consider using separate variables for calculations if direct arithmetic operations present challenges related to unit conversions or consistency. If issues persist, consulting SAP documentation or seeking support from SAP BPC experts can provide further insights tailored to your specific environment and requirements.

entry.title: Re: SAP SLT Create time stamp wiped out with record update.
entry.content:
Hi 

It seems like you're facing an issue where the 'Create time stamp' (`CRTTTM`) value gets wiped out when a record is updated (`IUUC_OPERAT_FLAG = 'U'`). You want to preserve the original create timestamp value in such cases. Here’s how you can modify your code to achieve that:

```abap
DATA: lv_name_create(30) TYPE c.
STATICS lv_Date TYPE sy-datum.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <ls_record> TYPE any,
<lv_crttm> TYPE any,
<lv_operation> TYPE any.

CONCATENATE '<wa_r_' i_p1 '>' INTO lv_name_create.
ASSIGN (lv_name_create) TO <ls_record>.

ASSIGN COMPONENT 'IUUC_OPERAT_FLAG' OF STRUCTURE <ls_record> TO <lv_operation>.

CASE <lv_operation>.
WHEN 'I'.
" Insert operation
ASSIGN COMPONENT 'CRTTTM' OF STRUCTURE <ls_record> TO <lv_crttm>.
IF sy-subrc = 0.
CONCATENATE sy-datum sy-uzeit INTO <lv_crttm>.
lv_date = <lv_crttm>.
ENDIF.
WHEN 'U'.
" Update operation
" Preserve the existing CRTTTM value
ASSIGN COMPONENT 'CRTTTM' OF STRUCTURE <ls_record> TO <lv_crttm>.
IF sy-subrc = 0.
" Check if lv_date has been initialized (for the first insert operation)
IF lv_date IS INITIAL.
lv_date = <lv_crttm>.
ELSE.
<lv_crttm> = lv_date.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.
ENDCASE.
```

### Explanation:

1. **Initialization and Assignments**: You're assigning the field `<ls_record>` dynamically based on `<wa_r_i_p1>`. This allows you to work with the fields of the structure dynamically.

2. **Handling Insert (`I`) Operation**:
- When the operation flag `<lv_operation>` is 'I', you fetch the current system date and time (`sy-datum` and `sy-uzeit`) and concatenate them into `<lv_crttm>`. This sets the create timestamp when a new record is inserted.

3. **Handling Update (`U`) Operation**:
- When the operation flag `<lv_operation>` is 'U', you need to preserve the existing create timestamp (`CRTTTM`). This is achieved by assigning `<lv_crttm>` to `lv_date` only if it hasn't been initialized yet (i.e., during the first insert operation).
- For subsequent updates, `<lv_crttm>` is set to `lv_date`, ensuring the original create timestamp is retained.

4. **Field Symbols and Error Handling**: Ensure that you handle errors (`sy-subrc`) appropriately after each `ASSIGN COMPONENT` statement to avoid runtime issues.

By implementing these changes, the create timestamp (`CRTTTM`) should now be preserved when records are updated (`U` operation), while still being set correctly during insertions (`I` operation). This approach leverages dynamic field assignments and conditional logic to achieve the desired behavior.

entry.title: Re: API for creating Communication Management
entry.content:
Creating Communication Users, Communication Systems, or Communication Arrangements in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition typically involves using the SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) service rather than direct APIs exposed by SAP. Here’s a breakdown of how you can achieve this:

1. **SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI)**: This service allows you to integrate various SAP and non-SAP systems, including SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Within CPI, you can create integration flows that involve creating Communication Users, Systems, and Arrangements.

2. **Integration Content Catalog**: SAP provides pre-packaged integration content in CPI, which includes pre-configured integration flows for various scenarios. These can be customized to fit your specific requirements, including the creation of Communication Users, Systems, and Arrangements.

3. **SAP API Business Hub**: While SAP API Business Hub primarily offers APIs for reading data, there are sometimes Integration Flow packages available that include creation scenarios. These can be imported into CPI and adjusted as needed.

4. **Custom Development**: If the standard integration flows do not meet your needs, you can develop custom integration flows using SAP’s Integration Suite tools. This involves using tools like SAP Integration Suite to create API proxies or custom APIs that can handle creation of Communication Users, Systems, and Arrangements.

5. **APIs for Administrative Tasks**: For more administrative tasks related to user and system management, you might also look into SAP’s Cloud Platform Identity Authentication Service (IAS), which provides APIs for user management and single sign-on capabilities.

To summarize, while direct APIs specifically for creating Communication Users, Systems, and Arrangements may not be readily exposed by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition itself, leveraging SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) is a robust approach. CPI provides the tools and capabilities necessary to orchestrate such integrations and create the required entities within your SAP landscape.

entry.title: Re: Create Extension Wizard to Create SAP UI5 Extension
entry.content:
Hi 

It sounds like you're trying to create an SAP Fiori UI5 extension project using SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), but you're not finding the option you need in the wizards available under "File -> New Project from Template". The SAP UI5 Adaptation Project isn't suitable for your requirements, so you're looking for an alternative to create an Extension Project.

Here are a few things to consider and steps you can take:

1. **Extension Project Availability**: SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) should provide a way to create SAP Fiori UI5 Extension Projects, typically through the SAP Fiori Dev Space. The option might not be directly under "File -> New Project from Template" as you described, but rather it could be accessed through a different route or view within BAS.

2. **Exploring Project Templates**: Sometimes, project templates for specific SAP Fiori extensions or UI5 projects may not be visible under the default project creation options. In BAS, explore other menu options or views that list all available templates or project types. Look for options related to Fiori or UI5 development specifically.

3. **SAP Fiori Dev Space**: Ensure that you are in the correct SAP Fiori Dev Space within BAS. Different Dev Spaces may offer different sets of templates and wizards. Switch to or verify that you are in the correct Dev Space where Fiori extension projects are supported.

4. **Consult SAP Documentation or Community**: If you're still unable to find the appropriate project template or wizard, consult the SAP documentation specific to BAS or Fiori development. Additionally, SAP Community forums or SAP support might provide insights or guidance from other developers who have encountered similar challenges.

5. **Custom Project Configuration**: If necessary, consider configuring a custom project in BAS by starting with a basic UI5 application template and then adapting it for extension purposes manually. This may involve setting up the necessary Fiori and UI5 dependencies, configurations, and folder structures.

In summary, while BAS should support creating SAP Fiori UI5 extension projects, the exact location or naming of the template might not be immediately obvious. Continue exploring BAS's options, ensure you're in the correct environment, and refer to SAP's documentation or community resources for additional assistance.

entry.title: Re: RAP (Fiori Elements) how to make a Criticality for Ac...
entry.content:
Hi 

In a RAP (Repository-Adaptive Programming) application using Fiori Elements, you can customize the appearance of action buttons based on certain conditions such as criticality or other business rules. Here’s how you can achieve this:

### 1. Using Annotations

Annotations in RAP applications allow you to define metadata that controls the behavior and appearance of UI elements, including action buttons.

#### a. Define Criticality Annotation

You can define a criticality annotation for an action button in your CDS (Core Data Services) view. Here's an example:

```abap
annotate YourEntity {

// Define a criticality annotation for action buttons
define action YourAction {
'@UI:Criticality' : {
Value : 'Negative' // Possible values: 'Positive', 'Critical', 'Negative', 'None'
}
}

}
```

In this annotation:

- `YourEntity` is the entity where your action (`YourAction`) is defined.
- `@UI:Criticality` specifies that you are defining the criticality for the action.
- `Value` specifies the criticality level, which can be 'Positive', 'Critical', 'Negative', or 'None'. This determines the color of the action button.

#### b. Fiori Elements Configuration

In your Fiori Elements annotations (usually defined in a separate file or embedded within CDS annotations), ensure you have included the necessary annotations for actions:

```abap
annotate YourEntity with @(
// Other annotations for entity configuration
UI.LineItem: [
{ Actions: [YourAction] }
]
)
```

### 2. Styling Action Buttons

To style action buttons based on criticality, Fiori Elements automatically applies predefined styles to buttons based on their criticality level:

- **Positive**: Typically shown in green.
- **Critical**: Typically shown in yellow.
- **Negative**: Typically shown in red.
- **None**: No specific color applied.

These styles are part of the Fiori design guidelines and are applied automatically based on the annotations you define.

### Example Scenario

If `YourAction` has a criticality defined as `Negative`, the action button associated with `YourAction` in your Fiori Elements application will automatically be styled with a red color, indicating its critical nature.

### Additional Considerations

- **Custom Styling**: If you need custom colors or styles beyond the standard Fiori Elements behavior, you may need to implement custom CSS or JavaScript overrides in your application, which could involve extending the Fiori Elements framework.

- **Testing**: Always test your annotations and configurations in a sandbox or development environment before deploying to production to ensure they behave as expected across different scenarios.

By leveraging annotations and understanding how Fiori Elements interprets these annotations, you can effectively control the criticality and appearance of action buttons in your RAP applications.

entry.title: Re: CAP: query historical data from entity with temporal...
entry.content:
Hi 

In SAP Core Data Services (CDS) with temporal aspects, querying for "older" or "invalid" entries typically involves using the `sap-valid-from` URL parameter as you mentioned. However, you want to target a specific entity or association without affecting all expanded entities.

Here are some approaches and considerations to achieve this:

### 1. Custom CDS Annotations

You can define custom annotations in your CDS definition to control the temporal querying behavior at the association level.

#### Example:

```abap
entity Tasks {
key ID: UUID;
// Other fields

// Association to ChildEntity
childEntity: association to ChildEntity {
// Define custom annotations
@CDS.valid.from.lowerBound: '0000-01-01' // Custom annotation for lower valid-from bound
@CDS.valid.to.upperBound: '9999-12-31' // Custom annotation for upper valid-to bound
};
}

entity ChildEntity {
key ID: UUID;
// Other fields

// Association to ChildEntity2
childEntity2: association to ChildEntity2 {
// Define custom annotations
@CDS.valid.from.lowerBound: '0000-01-01' // Custom annotation for lower valid-from bound
@CDS.valid.to.upperBound: '9999-12-31' // Custom annotation for upper valid-to bound
};
}

entity ChildEntity2 {
key ID: UUID;
// Other fields
}
```

In this example:

- `@cds.valid.from.lowerBound` sets a lower bound for the `sap-valid-from` parameter, effectively querying for "older" entries starting from a specific date (`0000-01-01`).
- `@cds.valid.to.upperBound` sets an upper bound for the `sap-valid-to` parameter, ensuring that the entries retrieved are considered "invalid" or "historical".

### 2. Using URL Parameters

While setting URL parameters (`sap-valid-from`, `sap-valid-to`) affects all expanded entities, you can control the temporal querying by setting these parameters specifically for the associations you are interested in.

#### Example URL:

```
/Tasks(123)?$expand=childEntity($expand=childEntity2)&sap-valid-from=date'0000-01-01'
```

This URL would fetch `Tasks` with ID `123` and expand `childEntity`, which in turn expands `childEntity2`, filtering for entries that are valid before the date `0000-01-01`.

### 3. Custom Handler

If the above methods do not meet your specific requirements, implementing a custom handler might be necessary. This involves creating custom logic to handle temporal queries based on more complex business rules or specific conditions not covered by standard CDS annotations or URL parameters.

### Conclusion

While SAP CDS provides robust mechanisms for temporal querying using annotations and URL parameters, customizing these approaches based on your specific entity and association needs is key. Using custom annotations and carefully crafting your URL queries can provide the flexibility to retrieve "older" or "invalid" entries efficiently without affecting all entities globally. If more complex logic is needed, leveraging custom handlers can extend the functionality further.

entry.title: Re: How to manage Decimal notation based on User profile...
entry.content:
In SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), managing decimal notation based on user preferences similar to SAP systems can be achieved to some extent through localization settings and data input options. SAC allows you to define how data is displayed and interpreted, including decimal and thousand separators, but it does not provide a direct user profile setting for individual preferences like SAP ERP systems do.

Here’s how you can manage decimal notation in SAC:

### 1. Data Input Options

SAC supports different data input formats that users can use when entering data:

- **Automatic Recognition**: SAC automatically recognizes different formats based on the user's browser settings. For example, if a user's browser is configured to use commas for decimals and dots for thousands separators, SAC will interpret data entry accordingly.

- **Manual Input Format**: Users can manually specify the format when entering data. SAC allows you to configure the decimal and thousand separators in the input templates and data models.

### 2. Localization Settings

You can configure localization settings at the SAC tenant level, which influence how numbers are displayed and interpreted across the entire environment:

- **Decimal Separator**: You can set the default decimal separator (dot or comma).
- **Thousands Separator**: You can set the default thousands separator (comma or dot).

These settings apply globally across all users and reports within the SAC tenant.

### 3. Input Templates

When designing input templates in SAC for data entry, you can explicitly define the format expected from users. This includes specifying:

- **Decimal Places**: Define how many decimal places are allowed or required.
- **Formatting**: Use symbols like commas or dots to guide users on how to enter data consistently.

### 4. User Education and Best Practices

Since SAC does not provide a direct user profile setting for decimal notation preferences, it’s essential to educate users on the expected data format and best practices for data entry. This ensures consistency in how data is entered and interpreted, especially when dealing with different regional or organizational norms.

### Conclusion

While SAC does not offer the exact user profile-based decimal notation customization found in SAP ERP systems, you can effectively manage decimal notation through careful configuration of input templates, localization settings, and user education. By setting clear expectations and leveraging SAC’s flexibility in data input formats, you can ensure that users enter data consistently and correctly according to your organization’s requirements.

entry.title: Re: Screen Exit for ES22 : Add Z*field to table Control
entry.content:
Hi 

To add a custom field (`ZZ*`) to the table control in transactions ES21 and ES22 (which use screen SAPLES20H screen 0200), you'll need to use a screen exit. In SAP, screen exits are typically implemented using function modules, and the BADI `SMOD_EMDK0001` is commonly used for enhancements related to transaction ES21 and ES22.

Here's a step-by-step guide on how to proceed:

### Step 1: Enhance the Structure

First, you need to enhance the structure `EVERH` to include your custom field (`ZZ*`). This field should already exist in the database table `EVERH`, so you'll enhance the structure to include it in the screen.

1. **Enhance Structure `EVERH`**:
- Use transaction code `SE11`.
- Display the structure `EVERH`.
- Add your custom field (`ZZ*`) to this structure. Ensure it is of the correct data type and length.

### Step 2: Enhance Screen SAPLES20H 0200

Next, you'll enhance the screen `0200` of program `SAPLES20H` to display your custom field in the table control.

1. **Navigate to Screen Painter**:
- Use transaction code `SE51`.
- Enter the screen number `0200` and program `SAPLES20H`.
- Edit the screen layout to include your custom field in the table control.

2. **Add Custom Field to Table Control**:
- Drag and drop a column into the table control area where you want your `ZZ*` field to appear.
- Configure the column properties to display the field from structure `EVERH`.

### Step 3: Implement Screen Exit using BADI `SMOD_EMDK0001`

Now, you'll implement the screen exit using the BADI `SMOD_EMDK0001`. This BADI allows you to add custom logic to the screen processing of ES21 and ES22.

1. **Implement the BADI `SMOD_EMDK0001`**:
- Use transaction code `SE18`.
- Enter the BADI name `SMOD_EMDK0001` and click on Create Implementation.
- Implement the methods provided by the BADI to include your custom logic for displaying and updating the `ZZ*` field in the screen.

### Step 4: Activate Changes and Testing

After making the enhancements and implementing the screen exit, activate your changes and test thoroughly to ensure everything works as expected:

1. **Activate Changes**:
- Activate the structure enhancement in `SE11`.
- Activate the screen layout changes in `SE51`.
- Activate your BADI implementation in `SE18`.

2. **Testing**:
- Test transactions ES21 and ES22 to verify that the `ZZ*` field appears correctly in the table control.
- Ensure that data entry and update operations involving the `ZZ*` field function correctly.

### Additional Tips:

- **Documentation**: Maintain documentation of your changes and enhancements for future reference.
- **Transport**: Ensure to transport your changes through the SAP transport management system (STMS) from development to production systems.

By following these steps, you should be able to successfully add the `ZZ*` field to the table control in transactions ES21 and ES22 using the screen exit approach with BADI `SMOD_EMDK0001`. This approach ensures that your customization integrates smoothly with the standard SAP functionality.

entry.title: How Businesses Should Prepare for a Process Audit: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
Process audits are pivotal for businesses aiming to ensure efficiency, compliance, and continuous improvement in their operations. Whether conducted internally or by external auditors, these assessments scrutinize organizational processes to identify strengths, weaknesses, and opportunities for enhancement. Here’s a detailed look at how businesses can effectively prepare for a process audit, the types of questions typically asked, common issues encountered, and strategies to ensure a successful audit outcome.

Photo by Scott Graham on Unsplash

Understanding Process Audits

Overall, the intention of a process audit is to provide assurance that organizational processes are well-designed, effectively implemented, and continuously improved to support the organization's strategic objectives and deliver value to stakeholders. It serves as a critical tool for maintaining and enhancing operational excellence, compliance, and overall business performance.

To do this, auditors will evaluate the adherence of operational processes to defined standards, regulations, as well as organizational policies. Audits may also encompass various areas such as quality management, compliance with industry standards, information security protocols, and more.

Steps to Prepare for a Process Audit

To help prepare, consider the following categories:

  1. Documentation Review:
  • Ensure all process documentation (i.e.: procedures, policies, manuals) is up-to-date, accessible, and aligned with current practices.
  • Verify that documentation accurately reflects the sequence of activities, responsibilities, and controls within each process.
  1. Training and Competence:
  • Confirm that personnel involved in audited processes are adequately trained, competent, and possess required certifications or qualifications.
  • Maintain records of training sessions, certifications, and assessments to demonstrate competence.
  1. Process Mapping and Flowcharts:
    • Create or update process maps and flowcharts to visually represent the responsibilities, sequence of steps, decision points, and interactions within each process.
    • Use these visual aids to illustrate process dependencies (i.e. templates, forms, systems, etc.), inputs, outputs, and controls.
  2. Performance Metrics and KPIs:
    • Define clear performance metrics and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for audited processes.
    • Regularly monitor and report on these metrics to track process performance and identify areas needing improvement.
  3. Risk Assessment and Mitigation:
    •  Conduct a thorough risk assessment for audited processes to identify potential vulnerabilities, hazards, or compliance risks.
    • Implement risk mitigation strategies and controls to minimize risks and ensure process resilience.
  4. Internal Audits and Reviews:
    • Perform regular internal audits or reviews of audited processes to proactively identify issues, non-conformities, or opportunities for improvement.
    • Address findings from internal audits promptly and implement corrective actions as necessary.

Types of Questions Typically Asked During a Process Audit

When auditors arrive, they will have many questions about your process. These questions are designed to assess the robustness and effectiveness of your processes, as well as your organization’s ability to adhere to them. Here are some common types of questions you (and your process performers) should be able to answer:

  • Process Understanding:
    • What is the purpose and objective of this process?
    • Can you describe the sequence of activities involved in this process?
    • How do you ensure that the process achieves its intended outcomes?
  • Documentation and Compliance:
    • Where can the documented procedures for this process be found?
    • When was the last time the process documentation was updated?
    •  How do you ensure compliance with relevant standards, regulations, or company policies?
  • Performance and Monitoring:
    • What are the key performance indicators (KPIs) for this process?
    • How frequently are these KPIs monitored and reported?
    • What actions are taken if performance targets are not met?
  • Risk Management:
    • What are the potential risks associated with this process?
    • How are these risks assessed and mitigated?
    • Have there been any incidents or issues related to this process? How were they addressed?
  • Continuous Improvement:
    • How do you identify opportunities for process improvement?
    • Can you provide examples of recent improvements made to this process?
    • How are suggestions or feedback for process improvement evaluated and implemented?

Strategies to Ensure a Successful Audit Outcome

To enhance the likelihood of passing a process audit with flying colors, consider the following strategies:

  • Proactive Preparation: Regularly update documentation, conduct internal audits, and ensure ongoing compliance with standards.
  • Clear Communication: Ensure stakeholders understand their roles, responsibilities, and the importance of audit readiness.
  • Training and Development: Invest in continuous training to maintain a skilled workforce and improve process execution.
  • Robust Monitoring: Implement robust monitoring systems for early detection of issues and proactive corrective actions.
    Embrace Feedback: Use audit findings constructively to drive improvements and strengthen processes over time.

As we have read, when preparing for process audits, it is important to address potential issues proactively, and demonstrate a commitment to continuous improvement. In doing so, businesses can not only pass audits successfully but also enhance operational efficiency and compliance in the long term. 

Process audits should be viewed, not as a necessary evil or an internal witch hunt for issues, but as valuable opportunities to refine operations, mitigate risks, and ultimately, achieve sustainable business success.

— — — — — — — — — — — -

If you found this article helpful, be sure to click like. And of course, follow me for more on the topics of AI, BPM, Process Mining, Customer Experience, Digital Transformation, and Automation.

Until next time — Keep Transforming 💡

entry.title: Re: Dividing different units in advanced formula
entry.content:
@carsten4711 

Using variables to store values from RESULTLOOKUP is not a good practice as these variables are additive and they are not cleared for each record. So if the code runs for 2 or months, all your variables will keep getting added. Using variables to store a constant value is a good use case.

Why not the below directly in line 19

DATA([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages", [d/Date] = "202412", [d/PD_Business_Area] = "CSO") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages", [d/Date] = "202411")/RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "FHeads_FJEmps", [d/Date] = "202411")

 

or even better to make it scalable. YYYY.PP average will be calculated using previous months salary and FTE

MEMBERSET [d/Account] = ("Opex - Salaries & Wages", "FHeads_FJEmps")
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "202411" TO "202412"
MEMBERSET [d/PD_Business_Area] = ("CSO")
MEMBERSET [d/PD_Cost_Center] = ("1000/#")

DATA([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "Opex - Salaries & Wages", [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(1))/RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account] = "FHeads_FJEmps", [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(1))

 

In above code 202411 will be calculated using 202410 and 202412 will be calculated using 202411

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Manage characterstics as custom fields in Fiori apps
entry.content:
Overview:

The business use case/ requirement which usually is requested by almost all organizations using variant configuration as a core solution in their processes is to have these VC characteristics very easily available across many reporting/monitoring tools specifically while monitoring production orders. As a result, we tend to add an enhancement in transactions COOIS/COHV to aid this. As Fiori’s adoption with S4 increases and with SAP recommending clean core to clients globally, we as consultants will need to fall back on low-code/no-code functionalities to manage such customer specific requirements. To address this requirement, we can use two Fiori apps in tandem to add these characteristics in Fiori app “manage production orders”. Manage production orders Fiori app can be used to monitor/manage the overall shop floor activities.

 

Pre-requisites:

  • Configurable Material master & other manufacturing related master data are already setup
  • Fiori apps “Manage Characteristics as custom fields(F4337)”, “Custom fields(F1481)”, “Manage production orders(F2336)” are already activated & required authorizations available
  • Configuration profile for KMAT has been setup with “Advanced variant configuration”
  • Sales order & production is created for the configurable material

Steps to create custom field & adding the field to Fiori app:

Step 1: Create characteristics as a custom field:

  • Open app Manage Characteristics as custom fields, for adding custom fields to Manage production order app, we need to use business context “PRODUCTION_ORDER_CFE”

 

  • Toggle to details & click on edit & add a characteristic, below example, I have added characteristics – COLOUR & save. After saving, wait for some time to allow the system to publish, the field status needs to change to “ready to use”

 

 

 

 

Step 2 – Extend data source to add the custom field:

  • Open Fiori app “Custom fields & Logic”, go to tab “data source extensions” & click on the plus button to add an extension

 

  • Add the field “Colour” by selection

 

  • Save & publish

 

Step 3: Display the added field in app “Manage production orders”

  • Open app manage production orders, now the column for the added charactrstics is available

 

 

Conclusion:

The Fiori app “manage characteristics as custom fields” is a powerful tool for the functional consultants to add in the fields in the Fiori apps. This app also provides a functionality to add batch characteristics to the app for managing batches, which is also will find many use cases in the batch managed production industries. For release information, restriction & issues regarding this app please refer to KBA 3220904.

I hope this blog will help in answering some business requirements. I would appreciate any suggestions or improvements to make this blog more productive.

Thanks, Ninad

entry.title: Sybase ASE 16.0.03.11 truncate table partition very slow when archiving data using NLS
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We are using NLS to archive data from SAP BW NetWeaver 7.4 SP 13 on Sybase ASE 16.0.03.11 to Sybase IQ.

When trying to archive an info provider which is a semantically partitioned object based on DSOs, we are running into an issue during the deletion phase of the archiving process. The copy and verification process are good, but during the deletion phase, the following statement takes around 7 hours to run per partition and the job takes days to finish. The strange thing is some of the partitions are empty and have no data as well and still it takes around 7 hours to finish.

TRUNCATE TABLE "xxxx" PARTITION "xxxx"

Can someone provide step by step instructions on how to go about investigating this issue. I would like to understand how to record traces on Sybase ASE 16 16.0.03.11 to understand what's causing this slowness on the database.

Thank you and appreciate any help on this !

entry.title: How to mass convert Quotations into Sales Orders
entry.content:
Daily, we have a huge amount of Quotations to be converted into Sales Orders. For this matter, we need a mass processing tool to convert them, because doing it one by one it's too time consuming.

How can we convert Sales Quotations into Sales Orders massively?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to mass convert Quotations into Sales Orders
entry.content:
There are 2 ways to mass create Sales Orders with reference to a Sales Quotation:

 

1. Using the Sales Order (A2A) API.

You can either create an independent sales order or create a sales order with reference to a preceding document.

For more details, check the documentation: Create Sales Orders with Reference | SAP Help Portal

 

2. Using the Import Sales Orders app

You can use this app to create sales orders from spreadsheets. You can import document data to create sales documents with reference to a preceding document.

For more details, check the documentation: Import Sales Orders | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: if you want to go a bit further than the SAP trainings on AI and understand how SAP JOULE works
entry.content:
If you are interested in all the innovations related to AI, you might also have attended the new trainings around SAP GenAI HUB  and SAP JOULE
And maybe you have also been asking yourself "Okay, but how does it work under the hood, what is the magic behind all this ?"

There are tons of content available freely on this topic, but the quality can be ..... limited (generated by ChatGPT ?)

So in case you are interested, here are the two best content I found for a technical explanations on the main concepts that SAP JOULE is based on:

- for a detailled introduction on LLMs (one hour but worth it  …): Intro to Large Language Models - YouTube

- to understand how SAP JOULE will add the context of the company data from their respective SAP systems and Microsoft applications, via a processus called Retrieval Augmented Generation ( or "grounding"), a short and very clear presentation by don woodlock: What is RAG? (Retrieval Augmented Generation) (youtube.com)

enjoy the show and please add your own finds !  🍿

(note: original posted on Linkedin)

entry.title: How to set a delivery block on schedule line
entry.content:
We need to insert a delivery block on the schedule line.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Re: How to set a delivery block on schedule line
entry.content:
1. Go to the app 'Change Sales Orders' (VA02)
  2. Select the desired document
  3. Select the item you want to block and click on 'Display Item Details'
  4. Go to the tab 'Schedule Lines'
  5. Select the Delivery Block on the table field
  6. Click save

entry.title: Divergência de volume - na emissão de solicitações de notas STO 861
entry.content:
Boa tarde pessoal!

Quando vou criar uma delivery, faço o Pedido de compra, depois o VL10B para gerar a delivery e em seguida o VL02N para fechar o processo e emitir a Nota Fiscal em J1BNFE.

Ao gerar a Nota Fiscal, o volume sempre vem a quantidade 1. 

Fiz 5 testes colocando o valor de quantidade do volume EX: 3, 5, 6 e etc.. e sempre na nota emitida a quantidade vem 1.

Alguem pode me ajudar de como consigo resolver este caso?

entry.title: Would it be possible to have the date format of DD/MM/YYYY available in SAP?
entry.content:
Hi,

Would it be possible to have the date format of DD/MM/YYYY available in SAP?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Would it be possible to have the date format of DD/MM...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Follow the steps below:

  1. On the upper right side, there's a profile button, the one that shows the user initial words. You need to click on this button
  2. Click on "Settings" 
  3. Click on "Language and Region"
  4. Check the "Date Format" field 
  5. You can choose "DD.MM.YYYY"

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Is it possible to report CBTA test results at configuration test case level on Composite scripts?
entry.content:
Is it possible to report CBTA test results at the configuration test case/script level with the individual configuration scripts being included in the Composite scripts?  I'm executing a Test Plan/Test Package automatically/immediately.

For example, say I have Composite script "ZCOMP_SAP_OTC_Create, change and display sales order" and the Test Script Steps include 1. VA01 - Create Sales Order, 2. VA02 Change Sales Order and 3. VA03 Display Sales Order.  When I execute all my scripts within a Test Plan/Test Package currently the test status reporting is from the composite level only.  So, if only the VA03 fails, then the test result that is report is an overall failure for the composite script.  In CBTA, is there a way to display results (pass/fail) for each of the test script steps with the composite script (in this example VA01, VA02 and VA03) instead of only reporting from the composite script level?

I've checked configurable reports, logs, Test Package Status tab, but I don't see a way...  Below is the Status tab of a Test Package that displays the composition scripts failed (no individual test case status), and this is how all the reporting seems to behave.

entry.title: loadProcedure is not a function

Hi,

I'm working with an MTA (multi-target application) within an on-premise HANA XSA with WebIDE. 

In my project I have a db module with a .hdbprocedure artifact and I'm trying to run it from a xsjs module.

In the xsjs module, I created a country.xsjs where I try to run the procedure. The code is like below:

 

var conn = $.hdb.getConnection();
var fnCreateCountry = conn.loadProcedure("TINYWORLD_HDI_TINYDB_1","tinyworld.tinydb::createCountry");
var result = fnCreateCountry();
conn.commit();
conn.close();

 

The problem when I run the country.xsjs I get the error below:

It seems that it is not finding the loadProcedure under $.hdb.getConnection...

I`m using HANA 2.0 SPS 07.

I did the exactly tinyworld exercise from the HANA developer guide:
SAP HANA Developer Guide for XS Advanced Model (SAP Web IDE) | SAP Help Portal

Appreciate any help on this.

Best Regards,

Thiago

#WebIDE, #HANA XS Advanced, #XSJS

entry.title: How to open a business object in Edit mode using a custom action/button via RAP/Fiori Elements?
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there a way to open the business object in Edit mode after clicking a custom action (e.g., Reset button)? The reset button currently has 2 purposes, first is to clear the screen and the other is to open the object in Edit Mode (i.e., Draft State).

I know that we can simply click the Edit button and subsequently click Reset button or vice versa, we are just wondering if it is feasible to do it in a single button click.

I tried applying the logic based on Ramjee's tutorial (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/abap-restful-application-programming-enabling-draft-feature-to-the-custom/bc-p/13750648) but I am getting this error message (although in the background, the draft instance has been created):

I suspect it has something to do with the URL parameters:

Before clicking Reset button, the UNAME field is filled up with the username value which is the key for this business object.

&sap-client=100#/zc_fileupload(Uname='RBALAGTA',IsActiveEntity=true)

However, after clicking the Reset button (and refreshing the screen via side effects), the uname field became blank.

&sap-client=100#/zc_fileupload(Uname='',IsActiveEntity=false)

Here is my code for the Reset action implementation:

 

   READ ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
        ENTITY Upload
           ALL FIELDS
          WITH CORRESPONDING #(  keys  )
        RESULT DATA(lt_file)
        ENTITY Upload BY \_Output
          ALL FIELDS
          WITH CORRESPONDING #(  keys  )
        RESULT DATA(lt_output).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
       ENTITY Upload
       EXECUTE edit FROM
       VALUE #( FOR <fs_active_key> IN keys WHERE ( %is_draft = if_abap_behv=>mk-off )
                                                  ( %key = <fs_active_key>-%key
                                                    %cid = 'RBALAGTA'
                                                    %param-preserve_changes = 'X' ) )
       REPORTED DATA(edit_reported)
       FAILED DATA(edit_failed)
       MAPPED DATA(edit_mapped).

    DATA(lt_keys) = keys.
    LOOP AT lt_keys ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_key>).
      <ls_key>-%is_draft = if_abap_behv=>mk-on.
    ENDLOOP.

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY Upload
         ALL FIELDS
        WITH CORRESPONDING #(  lt_keys  )
      RESULT lt_file.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY Upload
      UPDATE
        FIELDS ( Status
                 Input1
                 Input2
                 Input3
                 Input4
                 Input5
                 Attachment
                 Filename
                 Mimetype
                 Hideexcel
                 Updatedata
                 )
      WITH VALUE #( FOR <fs_rec_draft> IN lt_file ( %tky = <fs_rec_draft>-%tky
                                                    %is_draft = '01'
                                                     Status           = 'R'
                                                                             Input1           = ''
                                                                             Input2           = ''
                                                                             Input3           = ''
                                                                             Input4           = ''
                                                                             Input5           = ''
                                                                             Attachment       = ''
                                                                             Filename         = ''
                                                                             Mimetype         = ''
                                                                             Hideexcel        = ''
                                                                             Updatedata       = abap_false
                                                                             %control-Status  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input1  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input2  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input3  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input4  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Input5  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Attachment = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Filename  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Mimetype  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-Hideexcel  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                                             %control-UpdateData = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) )
      ENTITY Output
      DELETE FROM VALUE #( FOR ls_output IN lt_output ( %key = ls_output-%key ) )
                                     REPORTED edit_reported
                                     FAILED edit_failed
                                     MAPPED DATA(lt_updated).

    READ ENTITIES OF zi_fileupload IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY Upload
         ALL FIELDS
        WITH CORRESPONDING #(  lt_keys  )
      RESULT DATA(lt_new_file).

    result = VALUE #( FOR ls_file IN lt_new_file
                          ( %tky   = ls_file-%tky
                            %param = ls_file ) ).

 

 

 

I have added also the behavior definition below.a

 

managed implementation in class zbp_i_fileupload unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for zi_fileupload alias Upload
with unmanaged save with full data
draft table zdfileupload
etag master uname
lock master
total etag uname
authorization master ( global )
{

  field ( readonly ) Uname, Input6;
  field ( mandatory ) Input1;
  internal create;
  update;
  delete;
  association _output { internal create; with draft; }
  association _Parameters { create; with draft; }

  action processRecords result [1] entity zi_fileupload;
  action (lock : none) resetScreen result [1] entity zi_fileupload;
//  action (lock : none) resetScreen;

  validation validateInput on save { create; update; }
  validation validateInput1 on save { create; field Input1; }
  validation validateAttachment on save { create; field Attachment; }

  determine action valdetInput1 { validation validateInput1; }
  determine action valdetAttachment { validation validateAttachment; }

  side effects {
    action resetScreen affects $self, entity _output;
    action processRecords affects entity _output;
    determine action valdetInput1 executed on field Input1 affects messages;
    determine action valdetAttachment executed on field Attachment affects messages;
  }

  draft action Edit;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare
  {
    validation validateInput;
    validation ( always ) validateInput1;
    validation ( always ) validateAttachment;
  }

}

define behavior for zi_fileoutput alias Output
with additional save
persistent table zfilecontent
draft table zdfilecontent
lock dependent by _File
authorization dependent by _File
etag master uname
{
  internal update;
  internal delete;
  action processData result [0..*] $self;
  field ( readonly ) uname, sequence;
  association _File { with draft; }

  side effects {
    action processData affects $self;
  }
}

define behavior for zi_uploadparams alias Parameters
with unmanaged save with full data
draft table zduploadparams
lock dependent by _File
authorization dependent by _File
etag master uname
{
  internal update;
  internal delete;
  field ( readonly ) uname, sequence;
  association _File { with draft; }
}

entry.title: انوکھا۔" ٹیمو کوپن کوڈ {acu715892} $100 کی چھوٹ
entry.content:
آپ ٹیمو کوپن کوڈز {acu715892} کے ساتھ $100 کی رعایت حاصل کر سکتے ہیں۔ یہ خصوصی پیشکش موجودہ گاہکوں کے لیے ہے اور آپ کی کل خریداری پر $100 کی کمی کے لیے استعمال کی جا سکتی ہے۔ ڈسکاؤنٹ حاصل کرنے کے لیے چیک آؤٹ پر کوپن کوڈ {acq524571} درج کریں۔

SAP، اصل میں Systemanalyse Programmentwicklung (جرمن فار سسٹم اینالیسس پروگرام ڈویلپمنٹ) کا مخفف، انٹرپرائز سافٹ ویئر سلوشنز کا مترادف بن گیا ہے۔ جرمنی میں IBM کے پانچ سابق ملازمین کے ذریعہ 1972 میں قائم کیا گیا، SAP ایک کثیر القومی کمپنی بن گیا ہے، جس نے یہ شکل دی ہے کہ کس طرح کاروبار دنیا بھر میں اپنے کام کا انتظام کرتے ہیں۔

اس کے مرکز میں، SAP مختلف کاروباری افعال کو مربوط کرنے کے لیے ڈیزائن کردہ سافٹ ویئر ایپلی کیشنز کا ایک مجموعہ پیش کرتا ہے۔ فنانس، انسانی وسائل، انوینٹری، سیلز، اور کسٹمر سروس کے لیے علیحدہ محکموں والی کمپنی کا تصور کریں۔ روایتی طور پر، ہر محکمہ اپنا سافٹ ویئر استعمال کر سکتا ہے، ڈیٹا سائلوز اور ناکارہیاں پیدا کرتا ہے۔ SAP سافٹ ویئر ایک متحد پلیٹ فارم فراہم کر کے اس فرق کو پورا کرتا ہے جہاں تمام محکمے اہم معلومات تک رسائی اور اشتراک کر سکتے ہیں۔

SAP کی کامیابی کی بنیاد اس کا انٹرپرائز ریسورس پلاننگ (ERP) سافٹ ویئر ہے۔ ERP کاروبار کے مرکزی اعصابی نظام کے طور پر کام کرتا ہے، بنیادی عمل کو ہموار کرتا ہے جیسے:

مالیاتی انتظام: آمدنی، اخراجات، اور بجٹ کا سراغ لگائیں، مالیاتی کاموں کو خودکار کریں، اور حقیقی وقت کی مالی بصیرت حاصل کریں۔
سپلائی چین مینجمنٹ: انوینٹری کی سطحوں کا نظم کریں، خریداری کو بہتر بنائیں، اور سامان کی بروقت ترسیل کو یقینی بنائیں۔
ہیومن کیپیٹل مینجمنٹ: ملازمین کو بھرتی کریں اور جہاز میں رکھیں، پے رول اور فوائد کا انتظام کریں، اور ملازمین کی کارکردگی کو ٹریک کریں۔
کسٹمر ریلیشن شپ مینجمنٹ (CRM): کسٹمر کے تعاملات کو ٹریک کریں، سیلز پائپ لائنز کا نظم کریں، اور غیر معمولی کسٹمر سروس فراہم کریں۔
SAP کو لاگو کرنے کے فوائد بے شمار ہیں۔ SAP میز پر کیا لا سکتا ہے اس کی ایک جھلک یہ ہے:

بہتر کارکردگی: ہموار کام کے بہاؤ اور مرکزی ڈیٹا تیز تر عمل، خرابیوں میں کمی، اور پیداواری صلاحیت میں بہتری کا باعث بنتے ہیں۔
بہتر مرئیت: تمام محکموں میں ریئل ٹائم ڈیٹا تک رسائی درست معلومات کی بنیاد پر بہتر فیصلہ سازی کی اجازت دیتی ہے۔
تعاون میں اضافہ: محکمے کسٹمر ڈیٹا، انوینٹری کی سطحوں، اور مالی کارکردگی کے مشترکہ نقطہ نظر کے ساتھ زیادہ مؤثر طریقے سے تعاون کر سکتے ہیں۔
کم لاگت: ہموار عمل آپریشنل اخراجات کو کم کرتے ہیں، اور بہتر انوینٹری مینجمنٹ فضلہ کو کم کرتی ہے۔
اسکیل ایبلٹی: SAP سلوشنز آپ کے کاروبار کے ساتھ ترقی کر سکتے ہیں، مستقبل کی توسیع کو مدنظر رکھتے ہوئے۔
تاہم، SAP ایک ہی سائز میں فٹ ہونے والا حل نہیں ہے۔ کمپنی مختلف کاروباری ضروریات کو پورا کرنے والی مصنوعات کی ایک رینج پیش کرتی ہے۔ یہاں کچھ اہم پیش کشوں کی ایک خرابی ہے:

SAP S/4HANA: تیز تر پروسیسنگ اور حقیقی وقت کے تجزیات کے لیے ایک ان میموری ڈیٹا بیس پر بنایا گیا تازہ ترین ERP سوٹ۔
SAP Business One: چھوٹے اور درمیانے درجے کے کاروباروں کے لیے ڈیزائن کیا گیا ایک آسان ERP حل۔
SAP کلاؤڈ حل: SAP سافٹ ویئر کے کلاؤڈ پر مبنی ورژن جو زیادہ لچک اور توسیع پذیری کی پیشکش کرتے ہیں۔
صنعت کے لیے مخصوص حل: صحت کی دیکھ بھال، مینوفیکچرنگ، اور ریٹیل جیسی مخصوص صنعتوں کے لیے موزوں حل۔
SAP کو لاگو کرنا ایک پیچیدہ عمل ہو سکتا ہے، جس میں سافٹ ویئر، تربیت، اور جاری دیکھ بھال میں اہم سرمایہ کاری کی ضرورت ہوتی ہے۔ تاہم، بہتر کارکردگی، ڈیٹا پر مبنی فیصلہ سازی، اور بہتر تعاون کے ذریعے مسابقتی برتری حاصل کرنے کے خواہاں کاروباروں کے لیے، SAP ایک طاقتور ٹول ہے۔

جیسا کہ کاروباری منظر نامے کا ارتقا جاری ہے، SAP مسلسل جدت طرازی کر رہا ہے۔ کمپنی کلاؤڈ ٹیکنالوجیز، مصنوعی ذہانت، اور مشین لرننگ کو اپنا رہی ہے، اس بات کو یقینی بناتے ہوئے کہ اس کے حل ڈیجیٹل دور میں متعلقہ رہیں۔ چاہے آپ ایک چھوٹا سا سٹارٹ اپ ہو یا عالمی ادارہ، SAP آپ کے کاموں کو بہتر بنانے اور پائیدار ترقی حاصل کرنے کا ایک ممکنہ راستہ پیش کرتا ہے۔
